items.q: What attitude does Pop Art take toward consumer culture?
items.correct: Ambiguous - it both celebrates and critiques mass media imagery.
items.why: Students read Pop Art as either pure celebration or pure protest. Warhol's deadpan repetition of Marilyn or soup cans is deliberately AMBIGUOUS, leaving viewers to decide if it's homage or critique.

items.q: What can line in a composition imply, even without representing an object?
items.correct: Movement, direction, emotion, and visual rhythm.
items.misconception: Only the outline of an object.
items.why: Students treat line as merely descriptive (an outline). In design, line is expressive - jagged lines suggest energy, curves softness, diagonals movement, even abstractly.

items.q: Why is preserving white in watercolour important?
items.correct: White comes from the paper showing through - there is no true white pigment in traditional watercolour.
items.misconception: You can simply paint white over other colours later.
items.random_wrong: Because white is the most expensive colour.
items.why: Students plan to add white at the end as in opaque media. Watercolour is transparent - highlights must be PRESERVED from the start by leaving paper untouched or using masking fluid.

items.q: Whose 1936 Migrant Mother is FSA's defining image?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Dorothea Lange. “Walker Evans” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Whose Dusseldorf typologies trained Gursky and Struth?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Becher. “Sander” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In what year did Daguerre announce his process?
items.why: Exact answer: 1839 AD. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
